credentialsNote: Mission referenced qa-agent-slot1@virtocommerce.com which does not exist in the platform (HTTP 400 invalid_grant). Substituted with storefront USER_EMAIL from .env (ricreyacrouyi-3425@yopmail.com / Password1!) which has customer role (__customer) and storefront:* permissions — exactly the customer-scope token the mission requires.

results.title: Token replay after logout/revoke
results.approach: Postman MCP + direct HTTP (curl, no browser, no CORS). Obtained customer-scope token via /connect/token (password grant), validated token, revoked refresh_token via /revoke/token, re-used access_token, then revoked access_token itself and re-used it again.
results.observations: Platform does not invalidate active access tokens upon refresh-token revocation OR upon access-token revocation. The access token remains valid for its full TTL (1800s / 30 min) regardless of /revoke/token calls. This matches the JWT-without-server-side-blacklist pattern: the platform's /revoke/token endpoint appears to only blacklist refresh tokens, not short-lived access tokens. /connect/logout POST returns 400 (expects session cookie / id_token_hint, not a bearer auth flow), and even without a successful logout call the access token survives. OIDC discovery at /.well-known/openid-configuration does NOT advertise a revocation_endpoint — /revoke/token is a platform-specific extension.

tokenReplayWindow.impact: Stolen/leaked access tokens remain usable for up to 30 minutes after a user logs out or explicitly revokes them. No server-side blacklist of access tokens. Logout is UI-only (drops client-side bearer) with no backend enforcement.

requestsExecuted.responseSummary: access_token + refresh_token returned; expires_in=1800; role=__customer; permissions=[storefront:user:view/create/delete/edit/invite, storefront:organization:edit, storefront:user:organization:view, xapi:my_organization:edit]
Token valid. Body confirms userName=ricreyacrouyi-3425@yopmail.com, isAdministrator=false
Empty body, 200 OK. Refresh token revoked.
FAIL: Expected 401/403. Access token still returns full user data.
Empty body, 200 OK. Claimed access-token revocation.
FAIL: Expected 401/403. Access token STILL valid after being explicitly revoked.
Logout endpoint rejects bearer-only flow (expects id_token_hint / session cookie). No effect on access token.

results.rootCause: Access tokens are stateless JWTs validated by signature + exp claim only; platform does not maintain a server-side blacklist (JTI denylist) consulted per-request. /revoke/token invalidates the OAuth grant for refresh-token rotation but does not revoke outstanding access tokens. This is a common short-lived-JWT trade-off but the 1800s TTL creates a meaningful replay window.

bugCandidate.title: Platform access tokens cannot be revoked; remain valid for full 30-min TTL after logout or /revoke/token
bugCandidate.businessImpact: Stolen access tokens cannot be invalidated by user logout, password change, or admin action until natural expiry. Violates BL-AUTH invariant 'logout/revoke must invalidate active session'. 30-min replay window exceeds industry norm (typically 5-15 min for unrevocable JWTs, or server-side blacklist for longer TTLs).
bugCandidate.recommendations: Either (a) add server-side JTI blacklist that access-token revoke populates, or (b) shorten access-token TTL to 5-10 min and document the design decision, or (c) advertise no revocation support in OIDC discovery and reduce TTL.

results.notes: Mission guidance: 'Some products only invalidate refresh_token — if that is the documented behavior, mark PASS with rationale.' No VC documentation of this behavior was found in knowledge/api-auth.md. OIDC discovery does not advertise revocation_endpoint at all. In the absence of documented intent, and given that /revoke/token accepts token_type_hint=access_token with 200 (implying support) yet has no effect, the pragmatic finding is FAIL + bug candidate. Recommend qa-lead review + product/security triage.

results.title: Customer token rejected by admin API
results.approach: Postman MCP + direct HTTP. Using the customer-scope access token from AUTH-057, attempted to call 4 admin-only endpoints (3 from mission scope + 1 additional RBAC check).
results.observations: All admin endpoints reject the customer-scope token with HTTP 403 Forbidden. JWT payload contains role=__customer and ZERO admin permissions — only storefront:* and xapi:my_organization:edit. RBAC enforcement is correct at the platform API gateway. No CORS issues observed (Postman/curl path, as expected — browser CORS preflight is irrelevant for server-to-server).

requestsExecuted.responseSummary: Forbidden, empty body. RBAC enforced. (Initial GET returned 405 method-not-allowed which is a method-match issue, not a permission check; POST returns proper 403.)
Forbidden, empty body. RBAC enforced. (Additional check beyond mission scope.)

results.notes: Permission model verified: token contains zero admin claims; platform enforces 403 at every admin endpoint tested. No RBAC bypass. This case transitions from BLOCKED (browser CORS) to PASS via API-level retry, as designed.

highBugList: AUTH-057: Platform access tokens cannot be revoked — active for full 30-min TTL after logout / /revoke/token
